# Component Model

`IGuiComponent` is the framework contract. `GuiComponent` is the default base class. Custom components may implement `IGuiComponent` directly.

**`IGuiComponent` members:**
- `LayoutParameters` — size/margin/padding/positioning, consumed by layout pass.
- `RenderFragment` — stable delegate wrapping `BuildRenderTree`. Identity must not change.
- `Attach(handle)` — called once by framework after construction.
- `OnInitialized()` — first mount only.
- `OnParametersSet()` — every pass.
- `Render(context, bounds)` — per-frame draw, before children.
- `RenderOverlay(context, bounds)` — per-frame draw, after this component's own children, before the next sibling slot.
- `Measure(availW, availH)` — intrinsic-size hook for `FitContent`. Default `(0,0)`.

**Configuration lifecycle:** Configuration actions registered during a blueprint pass apply only to that pass. At the start of each pass, when `AddComponent<T>(key)` reaches an existing slot, the slot's frame is `Reset()` — its accumulated action chain is cleared. The user's `BuildRenderTree` then re-registers actions for the current pass via `.Configure(...)` / `.ConfigureLayout(...)` / inline named arguments. Each pass's actions run on the live instance during patch.

This means **per-pass values take effect immediately** — passing `width: 200` to `Add<T>` next pass will resize the slot. There is no first-pass-wins trap. Closures still work the same way (a `.Configure(c => c.X = field)` call reads the latest field value because the action is freshly registered each pass).

**Public API (extension methods):**
- Slot declaration (`RenderTreeBuilderExtensions`): `Add<T>(key, ...)`, `AddContainer(key, ..., background, content)`, `AddContainer<T>(key, ..., content)` (generic for `GuiContainer` subtypes), `AddLabel(key, text, font, ...)`. All accept layout as inline named parameters: `width`, `height`, `widthMode`, `heightMode`, `fill`, `margin`, `padding`, `direction`, `positioning`. `fill: true` is shorthand for setting both modes to `Fill`.
- Per-slot config (`ComponentBuilderExtensions`): `.Configure(Action<T>)` for arbitrary state, `.ConfigureLayout(Action<GuiComponentLayoutParameters>)` for layout state.
- `WithContent(fragment)` on `IGuiComponentBuilder<GuiContainer>` for setting `Content` after the fact (prefer the inline `content:` argument).
- `StateHasChanged()` — two-level convenience:
  - `IGuiRenderHandle.StateHasChanged(fragment)` — the actual interface method; schedules a scoped rebuild of the given fragment against its builder.
  - `GuiNode.StateHasChanged()` — protected wrapper; calls `RenderHandle.StateHasChanged(RenderFragment)`. Available to all `GuiNode` subclasses. Direct `IGuiComponent` implementors must call `renderHandle.StateHasChanged(renderFragment)` themselves after storing the handle from `Attach()`.
- `RequestRender()` — protected on `GuiNode`; marks the dialog surface dirty without scheduling a rebuild. Use when only `Render()`/`RenderOverlay()` state changed (e.g. a button's `_isPressed` visual) and `BuildRenderTree` would emit the same child declarations. Avoids the per-frame reconciliation cost for purely visual state.

`IGuiRenderTreeBuilder.AddComponent<T>` and `IGuiComponentBuilder<T>.AddConfigurationAction` are both `internal`; public registration goes through the `Add*` extension methods, configuration goes through `Configure`/`ConfigureLayout`.

**Cascading parameters:** Blazor-style ambient values published to a subtree.

- **Producer:** `builder.AddCascadingValue<T>(value, content, name?)` — a **scope operation**, not a component. No key, no layout parameters. Implemented as `PushCascadeScope` on `IGuiRenderTreeBuilder`: saves the current `CascadeChain`, pushes a new link with `(typeof(T), name, value)` boxed as `object?`, invokes `content`, then restores. No slot is allocated; the layout tree is unaffected.
- **Consumer (GuiNode helpers):** `protected T? GetCascadingValue<T>([string? name])`, `protected bool TryGetCascadingValue<T>([string? name,] out T value)`. Typically called in `OnParametersSet` to snapshot the value into a field. Available to all `GuiNode` subclasses, including layout-transparent wrappers.
- **Consumer (raw `IGuiComponent` implementors):** `IGuiRenderHandle.TryGetCascadingValue<T>([string? name,] out T value)`.
- **Resolution:** `(Type, Name)` exact match. Inner scopes shadow outer scopes with the same key. `null` name only matches `null` name. Walks innermost → root, returns first match.
- **Reactivity:** any ancestor reconcile recursively reconciles all descendants — consumers re-run `OnParametersSet` and naturally see the latest value. No subscriptions.
- **Internals:** `GuiRenderTreeBuilder` carries `CascadeChain` (immutable linked list of `(Type, string?, object?)` entries). `PushCascadeScope<T>` mutates it during the blueprint phase as a stack push/pop. `AddComponent<T>` snapshots `CascadeChain` onto the frame (`frame.CascadeChain = CascadeChain`) immediately after adding it to the frame list — so the snapshot is taken at the slot's lexical declaration site. During the patch phase, `slot.ChildBuilder.CascadeChain = frame.CascadeChain` propagates it. `RenderHandle` reads `_parentBuilder?.CascadeChain` live on each lookup — the root dialog handle has `parentBuilder = null`. `TryGet<T>` unboxes `object?` on match (one allocation for value types per lookup).

**`GuiDialog`:** extends `GuiComponent` with open/close lifecycle, Cairo surface, input routing, dispose. Set dimensions via `LayoutParameters.Width/Height` in subclass constructor body.

**Dialog lifecycle hooks:**
- `OnOpened()` — fires **synchronously inside `Open()`**, before the first reconcile pass. Child component instances do not yet exist; their `OnInitialized` runs on the next render frame. Use this for state setup that must precede the first `BuildRenderTree` (initializing fields that the build reads).
- `OnClosed()` — fires synchronously inside `Close()`, after the renderer is unregistered. Component instances still exist (they are not pruned by closing — only by `Dispose`).
- For a "first render done" hook, override `OnInitialized` on the dialog component itself — it runs once during the first reconcile, after the first `BuildRenderTree` is built.
